channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: More bark than bite? NASA insiders oddly relaxed about latest budget threats
item.description: NASA insiders are surprisingly calm about potential budget cuts from the Trump administration as the Artemis II mission progresses, despite concerns over lasting impacts. Their resilience reflects a familiarity with past funding threats.

item.title: IBM becomes first company to pay up under Trump administration's diversity blitz
item.description: IBM has settled for $17 million under the Trump administration's Civil Rights Fraud Initiative, becoming the first company to do so, while still contesting age discrimination claims. This settlement underscores the scrutiny on corporate diversity practices.

item.title: Coming soon: 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
item.description: A preview of the upcoming '10 Breakthrough Technologies' list highlights significant technologies expected to impact society, including AI and biotech. This annual compilation showcases the evolving landscape of technology.

item.title: Microsoft raises UK Surface prices as RAM crisis reaches the checkout
item.description: Microsoft has raised prices for its Surface devices in the UK by up to £220 due to a RAM supply crisis, reflecting broader economic pressures affecting tech pricing. This move aligns with similar price increases in the US.

item.title: Silicon Valley Is Spending Millions to Stop One of Its Own
item.description: Silicon Valley is reportedly investing heavily to counter Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee who is advocating for strict AI regulations and running for Congress. This reflects the industry's concern over regulatory changes.

item.title: The problem with thinking you’re part Neanderthal
item.description: The concept of having an 'inner Neanderthal' suggests that modern humans carry genetic traces from Neanderthals due to interbreeding thousands of years ago. This idea continues to spark interest in human evolutionary history.

item.title: Man suspected of Molotov attack on Sam Altman's home charged with attempted murder
item.description: A 20-year-old man has been charged with attempted murder for allegedly attacking OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home with a Molotov cocktail. He reportedly planned to harm individuals at the OpenAI office as well.

item.title: Britain gives Rolls-Royce the nod to sketch out its mini reactor future
item.description: The UK government has authorized Rolls-Royce to begin design work on small modular reactors (SMRs), although these are not expected to produce power until the mid-2030s. This initiative aims to advance nuclear energy technology.

item.title: Bringing people together at AI for the Economy Forum
item.description: The AI for the Economy Forum, featuring collaboration between Google and MIT, aims to explore innovation and adaptation in the evolving economic landscape. This event highlights the intersection of AI and economic development.

item.title: Microsoft sends Outlook Lite to the great inbox in the sky as memory costs skyrocket
item.description: Microsoft is retiring its Outlook Lite app due to rising memory costs, with the final shutdown set for May 25. This decision reflects the company's ongoing adjustments to its product offerings.

item.title: UK state bank considers lengthening disastrous IT program
item.description: The UK's state-backed savings bank, NS&I, is considering extending its troubled IT transformation program, which is already £1.3 billion over budget and four years late. This extension could push the completion timeline beyond eight years, raising concerns about project management and accountability.

item.title: When the IBM PC and shoulder pads were big, Japan led the chip industry. It's trying to get back there now
item.description: Japan's Rapidus is set to begin production of 2nm semiconductors next year, aiming to reclaim its former dominance in the chip industry as TSMC expands its presence in Japan. This move reflects Japan's efforts to revitalize its semiconductor sector amidst global competition.

item.title: watchr-mcp 0.3.0
item.description: The release of watchr-mcp 0.3.0 introduces a QA agent for iOS and Android, designed to work with Claude Code. This update enhances testing capabilities for mobile applications.

item.title: wavestreamer-langchain 0.10.2
item.description: Wavestreamer-langchain 0.10.2 provides LangChain tools for waveStreamer, enabling multi-agent capabilities for prediction, research, and surveys. This update enhances the functionality of LangChain agents.

item.title: stratum-mcp 0.2.26
item.description: Stratum MCP version 0.2.26 introduces a server for Claude Code, focusing on structured execution and typed contracts. This update aims to improve contract enforcement in programming.

item.title: Why Most Professionals Are Stuck at Level 1 of the AI Skill Ladder
item.description: The article discusses the AI Skill Ladder, a framework that categorizes professionals' AI proficiency levels, revealing that many remain at the basic level. This highlights the gap in advanced AI skills necessary for complex automation.

item.title: uipath-langchain-client 1.8.2
item.description: The uipath-langchain-client 1.8.2 update introduces LangChain-compatible chat models and embeddings for UiPath's LLM services, enhancing integration capabilities for developers.

item.title: Show HN: Digest – a Claude Code skill for your daily dev news digest
item.description: A new Claude Code skill named Digest offers a daily tech news digest sourced from Hacker News and Lobste.rs, providing developers with curated updates. It operates with zero dependencies and score-based filtering.

item.title: Meta builds AI version of Mark Zuckerberg to interact with staff
item.description: Meta is developing an AI version of Mark Zuckerberg to interact with staff, part of a broader initiative to create 'personal superintelligence.' This project aims to enhance internal communication and efficiency.

item.title: Monday: Hili dialogue
item.description: The article discusses a dialogue related to National Thomas Jefferson Day, reflecting on Jefferson's legacy amid contemporary views. It highlights ongoing debates about historical figures and their impact.

item.title: How I Automated My Basic Marketing Tasks with AI (And 4x’d My Efficiency)
item.description: The author shares how automating marketing tasks with AI increased their efficiency by four times, allowing more focus on strategic work. This shift highlights the potential of AI in enhancing productivity.

item.title: fitz-sage added to PyPI
item.description: A new tool, fitz-sage, has been added to the Python Package Index (PyPI), enabling quick and efficient knowledge retrieval without the need for complex infrastructure. This innovation simplifies access to information.

item.title: Save a massive $700 on this 4K-ready HP gaming PC with a 9800X3D and RTX 5070 Ti, now just $2,499 — discounted HP Omen 35L pre-built powerhouse ships with 32GB DDR5 RAM and a 1TB SSD
item.description: A significant discount on a high-performance HP Omen gaming PC is available, now priced at $2,499. The rig features top-tier components, making it a strong option for gamers.

item.title: Goldman Sachs posts higher profit on strength in dealmaking, equities trading
item.description: Goldman Sachs reported a rise in profits for the first quarter, driven by strong performance in dealmaking and equities trading, reflecting resilience in the financial sector.

item.title: Why an exec at biopharma giant Gilead Sciences prefers energy management over time management
item.description: Gilead Sciences executive Johanna Mercier emphasizes the importance of energy management over time management in leadership, aiming to maintain productivity in a demanding role.

item.title: Developer partially bypasses Google’s AI watermark, undermining detection India’s deepfake rules rely on
item.description: A developer has created a tool that partially circumvents Google's AI watermark, raising concerns about compliance with India's deepfake regulations. This could undermine detection efforts.

item.title: As AI agents scale in crypto, researchers warn of a critical security gap
item.description: Researchers have identified a critical security gap in AI systems used in cryptocurrency, where intermediary systems can access and alter data, posing risks to private keys and security.

item.title: Apple Developing Several Frame Styles for Smart Glasses
item.description: Apple is reportedly working on multiple frame styles for its upcoming smart glasses, indicating a significant step in its wearable technology development.

item.title: Will Some Programmers Become 'AI Babysitters'?
item.description: A discussion raises the question of whether programmers will become 'AI babysitters,' as AI tools allow non-experts to generate code, potentially changing the role of traditional programmers.

item.title: 1 Day 2024 EU AI Act Legislative Changes and Potential Impact on Healthcare Webinar: (ONLINE EVENT: June 23, 2026)
item.description: A webinar is scheduled to discuss legislative changes to the EU AI Act and its implications for healthcare, focusing on compliance and AI training opportunities.

item.title: The Love Story Trope You’re Obsessed With (According To Zodiac Sign)
item.description: The article explores which popular romance and fantasy tropes align with each zodiac sign, highlighting the unique preferences of each sign in storytelling. This playful analysis taps into astrology's influence on personal interests.

item.title: AirPods Pro 3 are back at one of the lowest prices to date if you need a spring Apple buds refresh
item.description: Apple's AirPods Pro 3 are currently available at one of their lowest prices, making it an attractive time for consumers to upgrade their audio experience. This deal is expected to last into the new work week.

item.title: Apple's AI Chief John Giannandrea Departs This Week
item.description: John Giannandrea, Apple's AI chief, is leaving the company as his stock vesting date approaches, marking the end of his tenure. His departure raises questions about the future direction of Apple's AI initiatives.

item.title: China went from uninvestable to unavoidable—and Hong Kong is cashing in with a slew of AI-centric IPOs
item.description: China's shift from being considered uninvestable to a key player in the global market is highlighted by a surge in AI-centric IPOs in Hong Kong. This trend is enhancing Hong Kong's status among global financial hubs.

item.title: causabi-geo added to PyPI
item.description: The article discusses the addition of causabi-geo to the Python Package Index (PyPI), aimed at improving how AI search engines interpret websites. This tool could enhance website visibility and search performance.

item.title: sirius-chat 0.20.0
item.description: Sirius-chat 0.20.0 is introduced as a multi-user RPG dialogue framework compatible with OpenAI providers, allowing for interactive storytelling experiences. This development could enhance collaborative gaming and AI interactions.

item.title: Previous James Bond’s Return in New Sequel Confirmed, Title and Release Date Revealed
item.description: The return of a previous James Bond actor has been confirmed, along with the title and release date of the new sequel. This news reignites interest in the iconic franchise and its future direction.

item.title: Dew Drop – April 13, 2026 (#4645)
item.description: The Dew Drop article covers various updates including Windows Terminal development and GitHub Copilot CLI for beginners, providing insights into ongoing tech advancements. It serves as a resource for developers looking to stay informed.

item.title: UQPAY Launches Enterprise-Grade Card Issuing Capabilities for AI Agents
item.description: UQPAY has launched enterprise-grade card issuing capabilities specifically designed for AI agents, which operate differently from humans in financial transactions. This innovation could streamline AI-driven financial processes.

item.title: Electric Theatre Collective Ramps Up Design Offering Adding Will Denning & James Britton
item.description: Electric Theatre Collective has enhanced its design leadership by appointing Will Denning as Creative Director and James Britton as Strategy Director, aiming to strengthen its design offerings. This move reflects the company's growth and ambition.

item.title: Article: The Spring Team on Spring Framework 7 and Spring Boot 4
item.description: The Spring team discussed major updates in Spring Framework 7 and Spring Boot 4, focusing on architectural improvements and resilience features. These advancements aim to enhance performance and user experience in software development.

item.title: Did South Carolina woman attract real flamingos to her yard with fake ones?
item.description: A South Carolina woman unintentionally attracted real flamingos to her yard by placing plastic ones, leading to her property being designated as a protected habitat. This incident highlights the impact of human actions on wildlife.

item.title: Top 5 Crypto Assets to Invest in April 2026: Best Cryptocurrencies for Maximum ROI
item.description: The article discusses potential cryptocurrency investments for April 2026, predicting a transformative year for the crypto market. It highlights key assets that could yield maximum returns.

item.title: sagan-xai 0.1.4
item.description: The article introduces the sagan-xai 0.1.4, a tool for explainable probabilistic ensemble trading using advanced AI techniques. It aims to enhance decision-making in trading strategies.

item.title: pyoptiwize added to PyPI
item.description: The pyoptiwize library has been added to PyPI, providing an optimization tutorial that emphasizes learning through examples. This resource aims to assist developers in understanding optimization techniques.

item.title: sagan-xai 0.1.3
item.description: The sagan-xai 0.1.3 is another version of the explainable probabilistic ensemble for trading, utilizing AI techniques for better market predictions. It builds on previous iterations to enhance trading strategies.

item.title: tensor-network-visualization 1.6.2
item.description: The tensor-network-visualization 1.6.2 library offers minimal visualizations for various tensor network libraries, aiding researchers and developers in visualizing complex data structures.

item.title: The Man Who Built NVIDIA (with Stephen Witt)
item.description: The article profiles Jensen Huang, CEO of NVIDIA, detailing his journey from a non-English speaking immigrant to a key figure in the AI revolution. His leadership has significantly influenced the tech industry.

item.title: We Are Social’s Monday Mashup #769
item.description: We Are Social's Monday Mashup covers recent updates in social media, including X's Voice Notes feature, Meta's new AI model, and YouTube's AI enhancements. These developments reflect ongoing innovation in the social media landscape.

item.title: tracetorch 0.16.4
item.description: The tracetorch 0.16.4 library is introduced as a powerful and ergonomic Spiking Neural Network (SNN) library for PyTorch, aimed at enhancing neural network modeling.
